free_text: Measurement and theoretical analysis of the excitation
functions for 3He induced reactions on natSn
High purity metallic Sn foil of 14.05um thick.
Cross sections were measured by using charged
particle activation method.
Activity measurement of the reaction products
was done by Gamma-spectrometry based on HPGe detector
Two stacked foils targets were irradiated with
3He beam of 25.5 and 25.6 MeV and 100 nA for 1 hr.
One stack contained Ti the other one Cu monitor foils.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the
polynomial approximation of Andersen ad Ziegler.
The irradiation was done on an extracted beam of
the ATOMKI cyclotron.
The activities of the produced radionuclides were
measured nondestructively without chemical separation.
The energy degradation as a function of depth in the
stack was calculated from the input energy and target
thicknesses.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the primary beam energy
was estimated as (+/-0.3 MeV) which increased due to
straggling effect continuously in each foil through
stack up 1.5 MeV.
Typical total uncertainty is between 11-14%.
number of bombarding particles (7%)
surface density of target atoms (3%)
detector efficiency (7%)
nuclear decay and branching data (3%)
statistical and peak fitting (3%)
The experimental results were compared to the
result calculated by the TENDL and EMPIRE theoretical
model codes.
Integral yields were calculated from the
fitted experimental cross sections.
